# Research cold-email drafts for Celina Lin

Review the employer posting and lab website before sending. Replace generic recipients with a specific PI or hiring manager when possible.

## cvusd - Chino Valley Unified School District — Moderate/Severe Special Education Secondary Teacher - UNIVERSITY INTERNS ACCEPTED - 2026/2027 School Year

**Subject:** Interest in Moderate/Severe Special Education Secondary Teacher - UNIVERSITY INTERNS ACCEPTED - 2026/2027 School Year — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear cvusd - Chino Valley Unified School District Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Moderate/Severe Special Education Secondary Teacher - UNIVERSITY INTERNS ACCEPTED - 2026/2027 School Year opportunity with cvusd - Chino Valley Unified School District at cvusd - Chino Valley Unified School District.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a463c730dd56c76cc2f78f4

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Enrollment Management Analyst opportunity with USF College of Nursing at USF College of Nursing.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. **Subject:** Interest in Lab Analyst - multiple opportunities available! — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear SGS Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Lab Analyst - multiple opportunities available! opportunity with SGS at SGS.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a453dd34f64ba41dcb4cb10

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

## HENSEL PHELPS — Field Engineer - Brigham Young University - Idaho

**Subject:** Interest in Field Engineer - Brigham Young University - Idaho — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear HENSEL PHELPS Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Field Engineer - Brigham Young University - Idaho opportunity with HENSEL PHELPS at HENSEL PHELPS.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a2a31a41de59e0682a836b4

**Subject:** Interest in Laboratory Technician - Environment Testing - Instrumental Water Quality- 3rd Shift — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Eurofins Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Laboratory Technician - Environment Testing - Instrumental Water Quality- 3rd Shift opportunity with Eurofins at Eurofins.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

**Subject:** Interest in Lab Eng. Technician — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear UST Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Lab Eng. Technician opportunity with UST at UST.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a44155065e80d3c99f2b7fc

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Laboratory Analyst opportunity with Onterris at Onterris.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

**Subject:** Interest in Student Brand Ambassador - University of California (UC) - Los Angeles — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Kaplan Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Student Brand Ambassador - University of California (UC) - Los Angeles opportunity with Kaplan at Kaplan.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a462be24f64ba41dcb5112e

I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Associate Financial Aid Advisor opportunity with Capella University at Capella University.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

**Subject:** Interest in Administrative Coordinator 1 — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Southeastern Louisiana University Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Administrative Coordinator 1 opportunity with Southeastern Louisiana University at Southeastern Louisiana University.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a4616b73dbab558e29a38e5

More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. ## Penn State University — Part-Time Automation Analyst (Haile)

**Subject:** Interest in Part-Time Automation Analyst (Haile) — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Penn State University Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Part-Time Automation Analyst (Haile) opportunity with Penn State University at Penn State University.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Dear Oregon Health & Science University Hiring Team,

Dear NASA Jet Propulsion Laboratory Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Engineering Applications Software Engineer opportunity with NASA Jet Propulsion Laboratory at NASA Jet Propulsion Laboratory.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a2af7c3c07d4b6ae1c484b6

---

I am reaching out about the Marketing Coordinator opportunity with California Institute of Advanced Management (CIAM) at California Institute of Advanced Management (CIAM).

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

## SchoolSpring — PARAEDUCATOR 2 / Probationary / 7 Hrs / Conestoga Middle School

**Subject:** Interest in PARAEDUCATOR 2 / Probationary / 7 Hrs / Conestoga Middle School — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear SchoolSpring Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the PARAEDUCATOR 2 / Probationary / 7 Hrs / Conestoga Middle School opportunity with SchoolSpring at SchoolSpring.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Celina Lin

In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

**Subject:** Interest in Long-Term Substitute Kindergarten- 2026/2027 — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear SchoolSpring Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Long-Term Substitute Kindergarten- 2026/2027 opportunity with SchoolSpring at SchoolSpring.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I am reaching out about the Instructional Specialist - PE / 22 hrs wk / SHORT TERM - Lupin Hill Elementary School - Testing: Thursdays at 9:00 am (except holidays) at the District Office opportunity with East Side Union High School District at East Side Union High School District.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

## Brightview Senior Living — Activities Assistant - Memory Care

**Subject:** Interest in Activities Assistant - Memory Care — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Brightview Senior Living Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Activities Assistant - Memory Care opportunity with Brightview Senior Living at Brightview Senior Living.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. ## SchoolSpring — PARAEDUCATOR 2 / Probationary / 7 Hrs / Montclair Elementary

**Subject:** Interest in PARAEDUCATOR 2 / Probationary / 7 Hrs / Montclair Elementary — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear SchoolSpring Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the PARAEDUCATOR 2 / Probationary / 7 Hrs / Montclair Elementary opportunity with SchoolSpring at SchoolSpring.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

I am reaching out about the Para, Early Childhood, 4.5 hrs/day, 178 days-afternoon program FOR THE 2026-2026 SCHOOL YEAR opportunity with SchoolSpring at SchoolSpring.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

## Banfield Pet Hospital — Client Service Coordinator - 005228

**Subject:** Interest in Client Service Coordinator - 005228 — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Banfield Pet Hospital Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Client Service Coordinator - 005228 opportunity with Banfield Pet Hospital at Banfield Pet Hospital.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Client Service Coordinator (Full Time): Regency 0299 opportunity with Banfield Pet Hospital at Banfield Pet Hospital.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Celina Lin

I am especially interested in the lab's use of single-cell and spatial transcriptomic data to connect molecular mechanisms with therapeutic questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, I have built reproducible genomics workflows and enjoy translating complex biological questions into clear, testable computational analyses.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://boards.greenhouse.io/10xgenomics/jobs/7995069

In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in bioinformatics, pipeline development, and an LLM-assisted competency-mapping project has made me especially interested in rigorous, interpretable AI for biomedical data.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I am especially interested in building scalable data systems that let research and clinical teams use biological data reliably. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, I have built API-driven and Snakemake-based pipelines, automated SQL workflows, and worked across both scientific and software contexts.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the フィールドサービスエンジニア / Field Service Engineer - 日本 opportunity with 10x Genomics at 10x Genomics.

I am especially interested in the lab's combination of neurotechnology, quantitative biology, and questions about brain-wide computation. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background spans biology, scientific programming, and reproducible data pipelines, and I am eager to apply that mix in a neuroscience setting.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://boards.greenhouse.io/10xgenomics/jobs/7414686

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the ECQ Engineer opportunity with Bristol Myers Squibb at Bristol Myers Squibb.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://bristolmyerssquibb.wd5.myworkdayjobs.com/BMS/job/Summit-West---NJ---US/ECQ-Engineer_R1603360

In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

## OpenAI — Data Scientist, Safety

**Subject:** Interest in Data Scientist, Safety — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear OpenAI Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Data Scientist, Safety opportunity with OpenAI at OpenAI.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. ## Natera — Clinical Field Specialist

**Subject:** Interest in Clinical Field Specialist — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Natera Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Clinical Field Specialist opportunity with Natera at Natera.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

## Natera — Staff Biostatistician (Analytical Validation)

**Subject:** Interest in Staff Biostatistician (Analytical Validation) — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Natera Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Staff Biostatistician (Analytical Validation) opportunity with Natera at Natera.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

## Natera — PSC Manager - Las Vegas, NV

**Subject:** Interest in PSC Manager - Las Vegas, NV — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Natera Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the PSC Manager - Las Vegas, NV opportunity with Natera at Natera.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Sr Associate SAP Architect - S/4Hana PP/DS opportunity with Amgen at Amgen.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://amgen.wd1.myworkdayjobs.com/Careers/job/India---Hyderabad/Sr-Associate-SAP-Architect---S-4Hana-PP-DS_R-230876
